description_long: This object consists of a group of vintage hardcover books, some closed and some open, arranged in a neat row. The closed books feature ornate, embossed covers in shades of brown and maroon, with decorative gold patterns and flourishes visible on the spines and covers. The open books reveal aged, yellowed pages with visible text, suggesting they are well-used and possibly antique. The covers of the closed books are textured and appear to be made of leather or faux leather, while the pages are thick and slightly uneven. The books vary slightly in size but are generally uniform, giving the collection a cohesive, classic appearance. This set would be commonly found in a library, study, or as part of a decorative urban installation such as a book exchange or outdoor reading nook.
description: A collection of vintage hardcover books, some open and some closed, with ornate brown and maroon covers and gold decorative spines. The open books display aged, yellowed pages with visible text.
description_view_0: Front view showing all books, with ornate covers and spines visible, and three open books displaying their pages.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the books and the layered arrangement, with open books' pages clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the plain backs of the books and the decorative backs of the open covers.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the book thickness and the open books' interiors.
attribute_spine_design: The spines are decorated with gold embossed patterns and flourishes on brown and maroon backgrounds. Some spines have ornate designs, but no visible text.
